# CRM Test Fixtures

Three native-dialect 3NF CRM source schemas for end-to-end SMT migration testing.
Each script creates the same 14-table CRM domain (companies, employees, customers,
products, orders, invoices, payments, tags, junctions, etc.) but uses idioms
specific to the source engine, plus a 15th standalone `type_smoke` table (issue
#46) with one column per type/length case the CRM domain doesn't exercise:
length boundaries (`VARCHAR(1)`, the 8000/4000 MSSQL inline ceilings, MAX/LOB
forms), legacy LOB types (`TEXT`/`NTEXT`/`IMAGE`), explicit datetime
fractional-second precisions, time-only columns, sized binary vs blob tiers,
and numeric precision boundaries (`NUMERIC(38,10)`, `DECIMAL(1,0)`, `MONEY`).
`TIMETZ` is deliberately not covered — MSSQL/MySQL have no tz-aware
time-of-day type, so it cannot round-trip without a by-design tz-class
failure. XML / hierarchyid / sql_variant / spatial stay out of scope.

| File                | Engine                     | Engine-specific features used                                       |
|---------------------|----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `crm_mssql.sql`     | SQL Server                 | `IDENTITY(1,1)`, `DATETIME2`, `DATETIMEOFFSET`, `UNIQUEIDENTIFIER` + `NEWID()`, `NVARCHAR(MAX)`, `BIT`, `AS (...) PERSISTED`, `GETUTCDATE()`, `SYSDATETIMEOFFSET()` |
| `crm_postgres.sql`  | PostgreSQL 13+             | `GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY`, `TIMESTAMPTZ`, `UUID` + `gen_random_uuid()`, `TEXT`, `JSONB`, `TEXT[]` arrays, `BOOLEAN`, `GENERATED ALWAYS AS (...) STORED`, regex `CHECK` |
| `crm_mysql.sql`     | MySQL 8.0+ / MariaDB 10.6+ | `AUTO_INCREMENT`, `DATETIME(6)`, `TIMESTAMP ... ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `ENUM`, `SET`, `JSON`, `GENERATED ... STORED`/`VIRTUAL`, `TINYINT(1)`, `MEDIUMTEXT`, `INT UNSIGNED`, `ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4` |

## Common shape

All three are 14 CRM tables (plus the standalone `type_smoke`) with 22 FKs in
the same logical shape:

```
companies → departments (self-ref)
          → employees (self-ref manager)
          → customers
          → tags
          → orders
          → ...
customers → customer_addresses (CASCADE)
          → contacts (CASCADE)
          → orders
products  → product_categories (self-ref)
orders    → order_items (composite PK, CASCADE)
          → invoices
invoices  → payments
customer_tags  composite-PK junction (CASCADE both sides)
```

Coverage exercised by every script:
- Composite PKs (`order_items`, `customer_tags`)
- Self-referencing FKs (`departments`, `employees`, `product_categories`)
- FK action mix: `CASCADE` / `SET NULL` / `NO ACTION`
- Single + composite `UNIQUE` constraints
- `CHECK` constraints (`IN`, `BETWEEN`, comparison, multi-column boolean, `UPPER()`)
- Audit columns + soft-delete patterns

## Loading

```bash
# SQL Server
docker exec mssql-bench /opt/mssql-tools18/bin/sqlcmd -S localhost -U sa -P 'TestPass2024' -C \
  -Q "CREATE DATABASE CRM_MSSQL;"
docker cp testdata/crm/crm_mssql.sql mssql-bench:/tmp/
docker exec mssql-bench /opt/mssql-tools18/bin/sqlcmd -S localhost -U sa -P 'TestPass2024' -C \
  -d CRM_MSSQL -i /tmp/crm_mssql.sql

# PostgreSQL
PGPASSWORD=TestPass2024 docker exec pg-bench createdb -U postgres crm_pg
docker cp testdata/crm/crm_postgres.sql pg-bench:/tmp/
PGPASSWORD=TestPass2024 docker exec pg-bench psql -U postgres -d crm_pg -f /tmp/crm_postgres.sql

# MySQL / MariaDB
docker exec mysql-test mysql -uroot -pTestPass2024 -e "CREATE DATABASE crm_mysql;"
docker cp testdata/crm/crm_mysql.sql mysql-test:/tmp/
docker exec mysql-test sh -c "mysql -uroot -pTestPass2024 crm_mysql < /tmp/crm_mysql.sql"
```

The integration-test docker containers are started by `make test-dbs-up` (postgres + mssql)
and `make mysql-test-up` (mysql).

## Migration permutation matrix

With these three sources you can drive the full source × target migration matrix
(any of the three engines as source → any of the three as target). Use `smt create`
with a config like `testdata/crm/configs/<src>-to-<tgt>.yaml` (configs not included —
each user's setup will differ).
